verdict: Worth doing. All three claims (two ts-fx duplicate pairs + node.ts hand-rolling) are real and verified against source, and they share one root cause: there is no single "build envelope -> send -> correlate id -> decode" primitive, so the logic is re-implemented in four places (sendMessage/sendMessageAsync in bridge-protocol, the call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance family in pyodide-io, sendWarmupRequest/executeWorkerCall in node, and partially in each transport's send). The id-correlation gap (A3) is a direct consequence: because no layer owns correlation, HttpIO and PyodideIO simply skip it while ProcessIO duplicates its own. Consolidating buys real safety (correlation enforced once) and removes ~250 lines. Scope note: I'd fold the optimized-node.ts and protocol.ts deletions in as cheap co-located cleanup, but keep the BoundedContext/RuntimeExecution split (B7) as a SEPARATE follow-up theme — it touches the class hierarchy of every transport and is not required to land the dedup. Do not over-reach into B7 here.
verifiedSignals: DUP PAIR 1 CONFIRMED: src/runtime/bridge-protocol.ts sendMessage(:284-308) and sendMessageAsync(:324-348) are identical except line 306 (codec.decodeResponse) vs 346 (codec.decodeResponseAsync). Callers: sendMessage only at :470 (getBridgeInfo); sendMessageAsync at :379,:405,:431,:451 (call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance).

DUP PAIR 2 CONFIRMED + WIDER: src/runtime/pyodide-io.ts call(:376),instantiate(:403),callMethod(:430),disposeInstance(:457) each independently build a ProtocolMessage {id: this.generateId(), protocol: PROTOCOL_ID, method, params}, JSON.stringify, await this.send(...,30000), then parseResponse(:515). Four copies, not two; ts-fx flagged the two highest-token-sim.

B6 CONFIRMED: src/runtime/node.ts sendWarmupRequest(:566-630) and executeWorkerCall(:695-769) each hand-roll JSON.stringify of {id, protocol: PROTOCOL_ID, ...}, worker.transport.send, JSON.parse, and bespoke envelope/error normalization (:606-629, :741-766) that duplicates SafeCodec.extractResultFromResponseEnvelope. They use a private warmupRequestId counter (:561-564) instead of BridgeProtocol's requestId.

A3 CONFIRMED: SafeCodec.decodeResponse(:528) / decodeResponseAsync(:580) / extractResultFromResponseEnvelope(:413-444) never read response.id. BridgeProtocol.sendMessage generates id (:290) but never forwards the expected id to decode. HttpIO.send returns response.text() verbatim (http-io.ts:186-187). PyodideIO.send checks response.id is an integer (pyodide-io.ts:342) but never compares to request id. Only ProcessIO correlates (process-io.ts:250 extractMessageId, :600-619 pending Map lookup).

B7 INTERACTION CONFIRMED: PooledTransport inherits 4 throwing RPC stubs call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance (pooled-transport.ts:216-262) from BoundedContext's RuntimeExecution contract (bounded-context.ts:577-607). PyodideIO is 'extends BoundedContext implements Transport' (pyodide-io.ts:217) so it carries BOTH the Transport.send AND the RPC family.
optimized-node.ts is a pure re-export shim (src/runtime/optimized-node.ts:22-25), not in package exports map, zero importers (grep found only its own docstring).

protocol.ts is 2 lines (TYWRAP_PROTOCOL, TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION). TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION imported by bridge-protocol.ts:24 and dead bridge-core.ts:4; TYWRAP_PROTOCOL only by dead bridge-core.ts. transport.ts:20 already exports PROTOCOL_ID='tywrap/1'.
ProtocolResponse.id is typed required-number (transport.ts:78) and Python always echoes msg_id (pyodide-io BOOTSTRAP_PYTHON :171,:179), so correlation has data to work with on all transports.
falsePositives: Digest frames the pyodide-io duplication as a 2-method pair (call vs instantiate). SOURCE shows it is a 4-method family (call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance) with identical structure; ts-fx only surfaced the highest-similarity pair. The fix must collapse all four, not two.

Digest lists node.ts sendWarmupRequest and executeWorkerCall as two functions to merge. SOURCE shows executeWorkerCall already takes a messages descriptor and is the more general form; sendWarmupRequest is a near-clone with hardcoded strings. They should collapse into ONE, but note executeWorkerCall is the better base, not a peer.

REVIEW.md A3 says HttpIO/PyodideIO 'don't correlate response.id to request.id' implying ProcessIO does it correctly at the same layer. SOURCE shows correlation is NOT a transport-layer contract at all today: ProcessIO correlates only as an internal necessity of stdout multiplexing (process-io.ts:600-619), and BridgeProtocol/SafeCodec never assert it. So the real fix is to OWN correlation in BridgeProtocol, not to bolt per-transport id checks onto HttpIO/PyodideIO (which would re-duplicate the logic the theme is trying to remove).
proposal:
Introduce a single private envelope primitive on BridgeProtocol and route every RPC through it; make id-correlation a BridgeProtocol responsibility (not per-transport).

(a) BridgeProtocol.dispatch<T>(message, { arrow: boolean, validate?, timeoutMs?, signal? }): builds the full ProtocolMessage with id+protocol via generateId(); calls this.execute(); inside, encodeRequest, transport.send, then a NEW codec entry point decodeResponse(payload, { expectedId, arrow }) that (1) parses, (2) asserts response.id === expectedId throwing BridgeProtocolError on mismatch, (3) runs the existing extractResultFromResponseEnvelope, (4) optionally awaits Arrow decode. sendMessage becomes dispatch(msg,{arrow:false}); sendMessageAsync becomes dispatch(msg,{arrow:true}). call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance/getBridgeInfo all call dispatch. Delete the two duplicate sendMessage* bodies (collapse to thin wrappers or inline).

(b) SafeCodec gains one decode core `decodeResponseEnvelope(payload, opts: { expectedId?: number; arrow: boolean })` that supersedes decodeResponse/decodeResponseAsync (keep thin sync/async public shims for back-compat or change signature — see breaking changes). Correlation (expectedId compare) lives HERE so all three bridges inherit it for free, since HttpBridge/PyodideBridge/NodeBridge all extend BridgeProtocol.

(c) PyodideIO: collapse call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance into one private `private dispatch<T>(method, params): Promise<T>` that builds the message, calls this.send, parseResponse. The four public methods become one-liners delegating to dispatch. (This is the transport's OWN convenience layer; note these methods are arguably vestigial — when PyodideIO is used via PyodideBridge, BridgeProtocol.dispatch is what runs, and PyodideIO is only the Transport. They exist because PyodideIO implements RuntimeExecution. The B7 follow-up removes this entirely.)

(d) node.ts: collapse sendWarmupRequest + executeWorkerCall into one `sendDirectWorkerCall(worker, message, timeoutMs, label)` that reuses SafeCodec.encodeRequest + the new decodeResponseEnvelope against a shared SafeCodec instance, so warmup goes through the SAME encode/decode/correlate path as production calls (using a per-worker id but the same codec). This is the direct realization of B6.

(e) Cheap co-located cleanup: delete optimized-node.ts (dead shim) and protocol.ts (fold TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION into transport.ts next to PROTOCOL_ID, e.g. PROTOCOL_VERSION), update the two live importers (bridge-protocol.ts:24, test/runtime_node.test.ts:14). protocol.ts's other consumer bridge-core.ts is itself dead (deleted by REVIEW B1).
breakingChanges: NEW: response id mismatch now throws BridgeProtocolError on ALL bridges. Previously HttpBridge/PyodideBridge silently accepted a response whose id != request id. Any server/mock returning a constant or wrong id will now fail. This is the intended A3 fix but is observable behavior change — call it out in the migration guide and update any test fixtures that return hardcoded ids.

If SafeCodec.decodeResponse/decodeResponseAsync signatures change to require an opts/expectedId argument, that breaks direct callers of SafeCodec (it is part of the over-broad index.ts barrel per REVIEW B4). Mitigation: keep the old zero-arg-id overloads as deprecated shims that skip correlation, OR (preferred pre-1.0) change the signature and enumerate it. Recommend changing the signature since correlation should not be optional.

src/runtime/optimized-node.ts deleted: the deep-import alias OptimizedNodeBridge / ProcessPoolOptions is removed. It is not in the package exports map and has zero importers, so impact is only on users doing unsupported deep imports. Already covered by REVIEW Quick Win 1 / G7 migration guide.

src/runtime/protocol.ts deleted and TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION re-homed into transport.ts (e.g. as PROTOCOL_VERSION). test/runtime_node.test.ts:14 import path changes. If TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION is part of the public barrel, the export name/path changes (pre-1.0 acceptable).

node.ts warmup error MESSAGES change wording: today sendWarmupRequest and executeWorkerCall emit bespoke strings ('returned malformed response envelope for request N', 'failed: <type>: <msg>'). Routing through SafeCodec.decodeResponseEnvelope yields SafeCodec/BridgeExecutionError wording instead. Any test asserting on the exact warmup error substring (test/runtime_node.test.ts) must be updated.

Warmup now applies full SafeCodec validation (special-float rejection, key validation, payload size limits) which the hand-rolled path skipped. A warmup command that previously slipped a NaN through could now throw. Intended hardening; enumerate it.
verificationPlan: 1. Characterization first: capture current behavior with a snapshot/golden of (a) bridge-protocol call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance/getBridgeInfo happy-path responses, (b) node warmup success + each error branch (invalid JSON, malformed envelope, python error, missing result), (c) pyodide call/instantiate/callMethod round-trips. 2. Refactor behind these tests; existing test/bridge-protocol.test.ts and test/runtime_node.test.ts are the guardrail. 3. ADD new tests proving the A3 correlation fix: feed HttpBridge and PyodideBridge a response whose id != request.id and assert BridgeProtocolError (the out_of_order_bridge fixture mentioned in REVIEW E6 can seed this); assert matching id still passes. 4. Re-run npm run check:all (format/lint/build/type tests/unit) and npm run test:python:suite:core to confirm the live Python path still round-trips. 5. Re-scan with ts-fx duplicates to confirm the two flagged pairs drop out and no new high-similarity pairs were introduced. 6. Grep to confirm zero remaining importers of optimized-node.js and protocol.js after deletion.
steps: Read/confirm SafeCodec.decodeResponse/decodeResponseAsync (safe-codec.ts:528,580) share extractResultFromResponseEnvelope; design a single private decodeEnvelopeCore(parsed, {expectedId, arrow}) and have both public methods plus a new id-aware path call it. Add expectedId comparison (throw BridgeProtocolError on mismatch) gated so a missing expectedId argument preserves old behavior only if you choose the shim route.

On BridgeProtocol add private dispatch<T>(message: Omit<ProtocolMessage,'id'|'protocol'>, opts: { arrow: boolean; validate?; timeoutMs?; signal? }): generate id once, build fullMessage, this.execute(encode -> transport.send -> codec.decode with expectedId=fullMessage.id and arrow flag).

Replace sendMessage (bridge-protocol.ts:284) and sendMessageAsync (:324) bodies with calls to dispatch({arrow:false}) / dispatch({arrow:true}); update call/instantiate/callMethod/disposeInstance (:373-457) and getBridgeInfo (:465) to use dispatch. Keep public method shapes identical.

In pyodide-io.ts add private dispatch<T>(method, params) that builds the ProtocolMessage (id via generateId, PROTOCOL_ID), JSON.stringify, this.send(...,30000), parseResponse; reduce call(:376)/instantiate(:403)/callMethod(:430)/disposeInstance(:457) to single delegating lines. (Note in code comment that these RuntimeExecution methods become removable under the B7 follow-up.)

In node.ts collapse sendWarmupRequest(:566) and executeWorkerCall(:695) into one sendDirectWorkerCall(worker, fullMessage, timeoutMs, label) that reuses a shared SafeCodec (encodeRequest + the new decode-with-expectedId) so warmup shares the production envelope path; thread a per-worker request id. Update createWarmupCallback(:638) and createWorkerReadyCallback(:666) to call it. Keep distinct error labels but let codec produce envelope/error normalization.
Add tests: HTTP and Pyodide id-mismatch -> BridgeProtocolError; id-match -> success. Update any warmup/error-message assertions in test/runtime_node.test.ts to the new SafeCodec-sourced wording.
Delete src/runtime/optimized-node.ts. Confirm no importers (already verified: none).

Delete src/runtime/protocol.ts; add PROTOCOL_VERSION (=1) to transport.ts next to PROTOCOL_ID; repoint bridge-protocol.ts:24 and test/runtime_node.test.ts:14. (bridge-core.ts is deleted separately by REVIEW B1; if not yet deleted, repoint it too.)
Run npm run check:all + python core suite + ts-fx duplicates re-scan; confirm the two flagged pairs are gone and behavior tests are green.
dependsOn: delete-dead-code (bridge-core.ts / protocol.ts removal — protocol.ts re-home is cleaner once bridge-core.ts is already gone; if dead-code theme lands first, this theme only repoints one live import)

trim-barrel (B4 — settling whether SafeCodec.decodeResponse* and TYWRAP_PROTOCOL_VERSION are public determines whether their signature/name changes are breaking; ideally trim the barrel before changing these signatures)
